channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: Australia’s Largest Green Hydrogen Project Secures Green Light
item.description: Orica Ltd. approved a major renewable hydrogen project, the biggest in Australia to reach the milestone.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Zhu, Ocean Hou

item.title: Eastern US Heat Wave Set to Collide With World Cup Matches
item.description: A dangerous heat wave is expected to engulf the eastern US in the coming days, threatening power grids just as a critical slate of World Cup football matches is scheduled heading into the Fourth of July weekend.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Rosenthal, Brian K Sullivan, John Ainger

item.title: Emergency Ordered for Largest US Power Grid as Record Heat Nears
item.description: The Trump administration has declared a power emergency for the nation’s largest energy grid in advance of a dangerous heat wave that threatens to strain electricity infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ari Natter

item.title: Carney Concedes Canada Will Overshoot Emissions Forecasts
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney said Canada’s emissions will be higher in the coming years than previously projected, his clearest admission yet that the country’s near-term climate targets are likely out of reach.
item.dc:creator: Laura Dhillon Kane

item.title: Air Products Axes Massive Clean Energy Complex in Louisiana
item.description: Air Products and Chemicals Inc. is scrapping plans to develop a multibillion-dollar project in Louisiana that would have produced of hydrogen and captured carbon dioxide.
item.dc:creator: Ari Natter, Simon Casey

item.title: EU Adds Palm Oil for Drugs, Soy Seeds to Deforestation Carve-Out
item.description: The EU plans to exempt palm oil derivatives used in medicines and soybeans used for sowing from its landmark rules tackling global deforestation in its latest attempt to blunt the law’s impact on sensitive sectors.
item.dc:creator: Max Ramsay, Ewa Krukowska

item.title: Spain’s Solar Is So Cheap Investors Are Looking for an Exit
item.description: The country has too much power on sunny days.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Gualtieri, Eamon Farhat, Clara Hernanz Lizarraga

item.title: France’s Far Right Vows Aircon for Masses as Heat Turns Political
item.description: France’s far right is promising air conditioners now, rather than climate solutions for later, to try and sway voters as Europe bakes under record temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Ania Nussbaum

item.title: Amazon to Buy Half the Carbon Credits From Bacon Tree Project
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. said it will buy just less than half of the carbon credits to be generated by a project to restore degraded land in South Africa by planting thickets of Spekboom, a plant that sequesters carbon dioxide and helps soil retain water.
item.dc:creator: Antony Sguazzin

item.title: UK Grid Operator Lifts Post-2030 Investment Need to £89 Billion
item.description: The UK’s electricity transmission network needs about £89 billion ($118 billion) of investment after 2030 to cope with rising demand and more renewable energy supplies, the grid operator said.
item.dc:creator: Eamon Farhat
